methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $25M to date. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises eight private peers, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Dronamics, with no public comparables available. The narrow peer set—filtered by stage, timing, and valuation tier—lacks sufficient datapoints to support a reliable median multiple approach.

Given data constraints, Stage-Progression methodology is the applicable triangulation technique. Using the $25M total raised as a baseline valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $50M. This method reflects normalized mark-up patterns across early-stage funding rounds in capital-intensive hardware sectors, though it relies on the assumption that prior rounds were priced at market.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median point of $50M. This range reflects ±15% tolerance around the stage-progression result, acknowledging single-method derivation. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of narrowly comparable peer multiples, limiting external calibration; (2) unit economics and customer traction visibility, which would refine the baseline; and (3) timing and market conditions relative to the previous raise. A fuller peer-backed analysis would require either adjacent-stage or cross-sector comparables with sufficient granularity.
